acts.summary: The primary legislation governing Alberta's K-12 education system. Establishes the right of access to education, the framework for programs of study, school governance, teacher qualifications, and education finance. Came into force September 1, 2019.

sections.title: Right of Access to Education
sections.description: Entitles every person aged 6-18 who is an Alberta resident to access an education program, establishing the foundational legal right to K-12 education.

services.name: Enrol your child in school
services.ms_name: Enrol and support your child in school

services.name: Get specialized learning support for your child
services.ms_name: Enrol and support your child in school

sections.title: Compulsory Education
sections.description: Requires every child aged 6 to under 17 to attend school, establishing the legal basis for compulsory enrolment in Alberta's education system.

services.name: Enrol your child in school
services.ms_name: Enrol and support your child in school

sections.title: Enrolment in School Operated by Board
sections.description: Establishes that a resident student is entitled to be enrolled in a school operated by the board of the school division in which the student resides.

services.name: Enrol your child in school
services.ms_name: Enrol and support your child in school

sections.title: Francophone Education
sections.description: Establishes the right to receive school instruction in French for individuals who have rights under section 23 of the Canadian Charter of Rights and Freedoms.

services.name: Enrol your child in school
services.ms_name: Enrol and support your child in school

sections.title: Courses, Programs of Study
sections.description: Authorizes the Minister to approve courses, programs of study, and instructional materials for use in schools, establishing the framework for provincial curriculum and assessment.

services.name: Find curriculum resources and programs of study
Explore career and technology studies (CTS)

services.name: Earn dual credit (high school + post-secondary)
services.ms_name: Explore careers and earn workplace credentials

services.name: Find curriculum resources and programs of study
Prepare for and write Provincial Achievement Tests
Prepare for and write diploma exams
Check assessment results and school performance data

sections.title: Alternative Programs
sections.description: Allows school boards to offer alternative programs that emphasize a particular language, culture, religion, or subject-matter or use a particular teaching philosophy.

services.name: Enrol your child in school
services.ms_name: Enrol and support your child in school

services.name: Enrol in distance or online learning (K-12)

sections.title: Home Education Programs
sections.description: Permits parents to provide home education programs for their children, subject to notification and supervision requirements through a school board.

sections.title: Early Childhood Services Programs
sections.description: Authorizes school boards and other operators to provide early childhood services programs (ECS/kindergarten) for children younger than 6 years of age.

services.name: Enrol your child in school
services.ms_name: Enrol and support your child in school

sections.title: Off-Campus Education Programs
sections.description: Authorizes school boards to offer off-campus education programs including work experience, the Registered Apprenticeship Program (RAP), and green certificate programs.

services.name: Earn credits through work experience or RAP
services.ms_name: Explore careers and earn workplace credentials

sections.title: Charter Schools
sections.description: Governs the application, establishment, operation, and oversight of charter schools in Alberta.

services.name: Enrol your child in school
services.ms_name: Enrol and support your child in school

sections.title: Independent Schools
sections.description: Governs the recognition, registration, and operation of independent schools, including accredited funded and registered unfunded categories.

services.name: Enrol your child in school
services.ms_name: Enrol and support your child in school

sections.title: Board Responsibilities
sections.description: Sets out the responsibilities of school boards including providing education programs, ensuring students with special needs receive appropriate support, and maintaining safe and caring school environments.

services.name: Get specialized learning support for your child
services.ms_name: Enrol and support your child in school

services.name: Get school mental health support
services.ms_name: Enrol and support your child in school

sections.title: School Council
sections.description: Requires every school to have a school council composed of parents, the principal, teachers, and community members to advise the principal and board on school matters.

services.name: Join your school council
services.ms_name: Enrol and support your child in school

sections.title: Transportation
sections.description: Requires school boards to provide transportation for students, with distance thresholds and eligibility criteria set by regulation.

services.name: Arrange student transportation
services.ms_name: Enrol and support your child in school

sections.title: Alberta School Foundation Fund
sections.description: Establishes the Alberta School Foundation Fund and the framework for education property tax requisitions and payments to boards, forming the financial basis of the public education system.

sections.title: Education Professions — Teacher Qualifications
sections.description: Establishes the Registrar, teacher qualification requirements, and the regulation-making authority for teaching certificates. The Minister, through the Registrar, issues certificates required to teach in Alberta.

services.name: Transfer teaching credentials from another jurisdiction

sections.title: Discipline of Teachers and Teacher Leaders
sections.description: Establishes the professional conduct and competency framework for teachers and teacher leaders, including the Alberta Teaching Profession Commissioner and complaint, investigation, and hearing processes.

acts.full_name: Early Learning and Child Care Act
acts.summary: Governs the licensing, operation, and oversight of child care programs in Alberta, including facility-based programs and family day homes. Establishes standards for the safety and well-being of children in licensed care and enforcement mechanisms for non-compliance.

sections.title: Facility-Based Licences
sections.description: Requires child care programs serving more than 6 children to hold a facility-based licence. Sets out application, issuance, renewal, and conditions requirements, plus inspection and monitoring authority.

services.name: License and monitor child care programs

sections.title: Enforcement and Oversight
sections.description: Establishes the statutory director's enforcement authority over licensed facility-based programs, including non-compliance notices, licence variation, conditions, suspension, and cancellation.

services.name: License and monitor child care programs

sections.title: Family Day Home Programs and Agencies
sections.description: Governs the licensing of family day home agencies that coordinate and oversee approved family day homes providing child care in a private residence.

services.name: License and monitor child care programs

sections.title: Regulations
sections.description: Authorizes the Minister to make regulations governing child care standards, subsidy eligibility, staff qualifications, and program requirements. Child care subsidies and inclusive child care standards are established through these regulations.

services.name: Find inclusive child care for a child with extra needs

acts.full_name: Skilled Trades and Apprenticeship Education Act
acts.summary: Replaces the former Apprenticeship and Industry Training Act. Governs apprenticeship education programs, industry training programs, and designated trades in Alberta. Relevant to Education and Childcare through the Registered Apprenticeship Program (RAP), which allows high school students to begin apprenticeship training while earning credits.

sections.title: Apprenticeship Education Programs
sections.description: Authorizes the Minister to establish apprenticeship education programs providing classroom and on-the-job instruction, engage post-secondary institutions, and issue credentials — the legislative basis for the Registered Apprenticeship Program (RAP) pathway for high school students.

services.name: Earn credits through work experience or RAP
services.ms_name: Explore careers and earn workplace credentials

acts.summary: Establishes The Alberta Teachers' Association (ATA) as the professional organization for teachers and governs compulsory membership requirements. Employment as a teacher by a school board is conditional on ATA membership. Teacher discipline provisions (formerly ss.16-61) were repealed in 2022 and moved to Education Act Part 7 Div 3.1.

sections.title: ATA Objects and Compulsory Membership
sections.description: Section 4 establishes the ATA's objects including advancing education and improving the teaching profession. Section 5 requires teachers employed by school boards to be active ATA members as a condition of employment.
